example:
I had to bite my tongue.
Bite your tongue and listen.
She bit her tongue politely.

meaning: end a disagreement by talking openly
example:
Let's clear the air.
The chat cleared the air.
We need to clear the air.

meaning: be less strict or critical with someone
example:
Cut him some slack, he's new.
Cut me some slack.
They cut her some slack.

All that work went down the drain.
Our savings went down the drain.
Don't let it go down the drain.

meaning: become too excited or lose control
example:
I got carried away shopping.
Don't get carried away.
He got carried away with details.

meaning: stay strong in a hard situation
example:
Hang in there, it'll improve.
Just hang in there.
She told me to hang in there.

term: have a lot on your plate
meaning: have many things to deal with
example:
She has a lot on her plate.
I've got a lot on my plate.
He has a lot on his plate right now.

They kept us in the dark.
I'm in the dark about it.
We were left in the dark.

He kept his cool.
Keep your cool in the interview.
She kept her cool throughout.

meaning: mentioned last but just as important
example:
Last but not least, thank you all.
And last but not least, the winner.
Last but not least, stay safe.

Please don't make a scene.
He made a scene at dinner.
She made quite a scene.

Keep training; no pain, no gain.
No pain, no gain, they say.
Remember: no pain, no gain.

We're not out of the woods yet.
She's finally out of the woods.
The firm is out of the woods.

She was over the moon.
I'm over the moon about it.
They were over the moon.

They pulled the plug on the project.
The bank pulled the plug.
Don't pull the plug yet.

I hate to rain on your parade.
Don't rain on her parade.
He rained on my parade.

I saw red when he lied.
She saw red at the news.
It made him see red.

meaning: wait until the next day to decide
example:
Let me sleep on it.
Sleep on it before you choose.
I'll sleep on it.

meaning: change to a better way of behaving
example:
He turned over a new leaf.
I want to turn over a new leaf.
She's turned over a new leaf.

Our plans are up in the air.
It's all up in the air.
The date is still up in the air.
